Clinic.title: Clinic
Clinic.description: Represents a medical clinic where healthcare providers practice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Clinic entity.

name.description: The official name of the clinic.

address.description: The street address of the clinic.

city.description: The city where the clinic is located.

state.description: The state where the clinic is located.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the clinic's location.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the clinic.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the clinic.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the clinic record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the clinic record was last updated.

Provider.title: Provider
Provider.description: Represents a medical doctor or other healthcare provider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Provider entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the provider.

lastName.description: The last name of the provider.

specialty.description: The medical specialty of the provider (e.g., Cardiology, Pediatrics).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the provider record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the provider record was last updated.

CoverageEvent.title: CoverageEvent
CoverageEvent.description: Records a specific healthcare coverage event or call, detailing interaction with a provider at a clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoverageEvent entity.

coverageType.description: The type of coverage provided, e.g., 'Inbase' or 'Outbase'.

coverageDate.description: The date and time when the coverage event occurred.

notes.description: Optional notes or additional details about the coverage event.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the coverage event record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the coverage event record was last updated.

Photo.title: Photo
Photo.description: Stores information about a photo uploaded in relation to a coverage event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Photo entity.

url.description: The URL where the photo image is stored.

description.description: An optional description of the photo.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the photo was uploaded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the photo record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the photo record was last updated.

Signature.title: Signature
Signature.description: Stores information about a captured digital signature related to a coverage event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Signature entity.

url.description: The URL where the signature image or data is stored.

signedAt.description: Timestamp when the signature was captured.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the signature record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the signature record was last updated.

definition.description: Collection for all medical clinics. Documents are typically publicly readable for lookup by authenticated providers, with write access restricted to administrators. Authorization is independent for this top-level entity.

params.name: clinicId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific clinic.

definition.description: Collection for all healthcare providers. Each document represents a provider, where 'providerId' is expected to match 'request.auth.uid' for direct ownership. Providers can manage their own profile. Includes 'clinicId' to link to their associated clinic.

params.name: providerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific provider, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Subcollection storing coverage events associated with a specific provider. Access is path-based, meaning 'request.auth.uid' must match 'providerId' in the path. The document itself includes denormalized 'providerId' and 'clinicId' for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks without fetching parent documents. This structure enables secure 'list' operations for a provider's events.

params.name: providerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the provider who owns this coverage event, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: coverageEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific coverage event.

definition.description: Subcollection storing photos related to a specific coverage event. Access is inherited from the parent coverage event. Includes denormalized 'providerId' and 'clinicId' for authorization independence, allowing security rules to directly verify ownership without 'get()' operations on parent documents.

params.name: providerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the provider who owns the parent coverage event.

params.name: coverageEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent coverage event.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific photo.

definition.description: Subcollection storing signatures related to a specific coverage event. Access is inherited from the parent coverage event. Includes denormalized 'providerId' and 'clinicId' for authorization independence, allowing security rules to directly verify ownership without 'get()' operations on parent documents.

params.name: providerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the provider who owns the parent coverage event.

params.name: coverageEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent coverage event.

params.name: signatureId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific signature.

firestore.reasoning:
The application error reports 'Missing or insufficient permissions: ... path: /databases/(default)/documents/timeLogs' with 'auth: null'. This indicates an attempt to list a top-level collection named 'timeLogs' when the user is not authenticated. Furthermore, 'timeLogs' is not defined in the provided entity schemas. Based on the app requirements ('Master List & Call Summary') and the 'CoverageEvent' entity description ('Records a specific healthcare coverage event or call'), it is inferred that 'timeLogs' refers to 'CoverageEvent's.

The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and secure `list` operations (QAPs) to resolve such permission errors and ensure future scalability and debuggability. The core strategy involves structuring data hierarchically based on ownership and denormalizing critical authorization context.

1.  **Clinics (`/clinics/{clinicId}`)**: Clinics are independent entities. They can be generally accessible for lookup by authenticated users, with creation/modification restricted to administrative roles.

2.  **Providers (`/providers/{providerId}`)**: Providers are assumed to be the primary users of the system, with `providerId` directly mapping to `request.auth.uid`. Each provider manages their own profile. The `clinicId` field within the Provider document (already part of the schema) serves to link a provider to their clinic.

3.  **Coverage Events (`/providers/{providerId}/coverageEvents/{coverageEventId}`)**: This is the critical change addressing the reported error. Instead of a top-level 'timeLogs' (or 'coverageEvents') collection, these are nested under the owning Provider. This fulfills the **Authorization Independence** and **QAPs** principles:
    *   **QAPs (Secure List Operations)**: By nesting under `/providers/{providerId}`, a provider can securely query only their own coverage events by requesting `/providers/{request.auth.uid}/coverageEvents`. This path-based authorization intrinsically filters results by the authenticated user, making security rules simple and efficient for `list` operations.
    *   **Authorization Independence**: The `CoverageEvent` entity already contains `providerId` and `clinicId`. These fields are denormalized from their logical parents, allowing security rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.providerId` and validate `clinicId` without performing `get()` operations on parent `Provider` or `Clinic` documents. This makes individual `CoverageEvent` document access decisions atomic and easy to debug.

4.  **Photos and Signatures (`/providers/{providerId}/coverageEvents/{coverageEventId}/photos/{photoId}` and `/providers/{providerId}/coverageEvents/{coverageEventId}/signatures/{signatureId}`)**: These are nested further under their respective `CoverageEvent`. To maintain **Authorization Independence** throughout the hierarchy, `providerId` and `clinicId` (from the parent `CoverageEvent`) MUST be denormalized into each `Photo` and `Signature` document. This allows security rules for photos and signatures to verify ownership directly (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.providerId`) without needing to fetch their parent `CoverageEvent` or `Provider` document.

This structure ensures clear ownership, enables robust `list` operations, and minimizes the complexity of security rules by leveraging path-based authorization and strategic denormalization.
